input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition that keys off the `$(IsTestProject)` property (set automatically by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) or, more simply, a naming convention. The cleanest approach for MTP migration:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>

  <!-- Shared properties for all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Only applies when the project name ends with ".Tests" -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

**Alternative conditions** depending on your convention:

| Convention | Condition |
|---|---|
| `*.Tests.csproj` | `$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))` |
| `*.Test.csproj` | `$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Test'))` |
| Either | `$(MSBuildProjectName.Contains('Test'))` |
| Explicit opt-in | `'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'` ¹ |

¹ `IsTestProject` is set by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`. If you use it, the condition must go in **`Directory.Build.targets`** instead (so it evaluates *after* NuGet package props are imported).

The **naming-convention approach in `.props`** is the simplest and most reliable since it doesn't depend on import ordering.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that MSTest-specific Microsoft Testing Platform (MTP) properties only apply to test projects, not production/library projects.

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conditioning. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of the MSTest package reference or a test-project convention.

The most robust approach is to condition on whether the project references `MSTest` (or `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`). You can use the `$(IsTestProject)` property that the MSTest SDK sets, or condition on the project file name convention.


I'll provide a straightforward answer with the best practices for this scenario.
